title: slew~

methods.description: sets new start point and goes back to target

body: [slew~] takes an amplitude limit per second and an incoming value to be 'slew limited'. It then generates a line towards the incoming value. The difference between [slew~] and [glide~] is that [slew~] has a fixed speed, not a fixed period. A limit of zero stops the line generation and a negative value turns the limiter off.
